description: Nico, co-founder of Elusive, discusses the importance of privacy in Web3 and introduces the concept of composable privacy using sandwiching. He explains the challenges of achieving privacy in decentralized systems compared to centralized ones, emphasizing the role of zero-knowledge proofs (ZK) in enabling privacy without revealing sensitive information. Nico illustrates this with an example involving Waldo, demonstrating how ZK can prove knowledge of information without disclosing it. He also addresses the implications of shielded pools on liquidity and composability in blockchain applications.
topic_summary: Nico from Elusive explores composable privacy in Web3, detailing the necessity of privacy, the challenges posed by decentralization, and the utility of zero-knowledge proofs. He highlights the trade-offs of using shielded pools in blockchain ecosystems, particularly regarding liquidity fragmentation and the need for smart contract modifications.
